data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me build the full ML.NET-based ticket classifier. I need to:

1. Add the ML.NET NuGet package
2. Create the data models (TicketData, TicketPrediction)
3. Create a model training service that trains, evaluates, and saves the model
4. Create a prediction endpoint controller
5. Wire everything up in Program.cs
6. Add more training data to make the model more robust

Let me plan the file structure:
- Models/TicketData.cs - input data class
- Models/TicketPrediction.cs - prediction output class


- Services/TicketModelTrainer.cs - training + evaluation service
- Controllers/TicketController.cs - prediction endpoint
- Program.cs - updated with DI setup

The CSV only has 6 rows which is very small. I should add more training data to make the model work better. Let me implement this.

data.content:
Everything works. Here's the implementation summary:

### Files created/modified

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Models/TicketData.cs` | ML.NET input/output data classes with `[LoadColumn]` attributes |
| `Models/TicketRequest.cs` | API request/response DTOs |
| `Services/TicketModelService.cs` | Model training (SDCA Maximum Entropy), evaluation, persistence, and prediction |
| `Controllers/TicketsController.cs` | Two endpoints: `POST /api/tickets/train` and `POST /api/tickets/predict` |
| `Data/tickets.csv` | Expanded from 6 → 42 training samples across all 3 categories |
| `Program.cs` | Added `TicketModelService` to DI |

### Endpoints

- **`POST /api/tickets/train`** — Trains the model, saves it to `Data/ticket_model.zip`, returns evaluation metrics (macro/micro accuracy, log-loss)
- **`POST /api/tickets/predict`** — Accepts `{ subject, description, priority }`, returns predicted category + per-class confidence scores

### Pipeline
Text featurization (Subject + Description) → concatenated with Priority → MinMax normalization → **SDCA Maximum Entropy** multiclass classifier.

All three test predictions returned correct categories with high confidence (Billing: 98.6%, Technical: 86%, General: 99.9%). Input validation rejects empty fields and out-of-range priority values.
